problem: One codex §9 [P2] on src/python/eval/s15a_embedder_probe.py. Frozen design §5.4 and the emitted caveat both promise that a projected_eu7 bootstrap CI is reported for transparency, but the code recorded only the projected_eu7 POINT estimate (~line 1269) per OK candidate; no CI was emitted. The GATE (probe_15a_pass) correctly consumes the POINT (projected_eu7 >= 0.90); this finding is purely the MISSING transparency CI in the artifact.
fix_summary: Added a deterministic projected_eu7 transparency bootstrap CI computed over the SAME per-query 1-bit-vs-f32 recall@10 series the gated point summarizes. Refactored projected_eu7 into projected_eu7_recalls(...) -> list[float] (the per-query series) + projected_eu7(...) returning float(np.mean(series)) (gated point, behavior identical). run_probe now stores the per-query series in per_model_raw and reuses the EXISTING paired_bootstrap_ci helper (single-series form: zero baseline, fixed bootstrap_seed, same n_resamples, percentile method) to produce {lo, hi, point}; the point IS mean(recalls) == raw['projected_eu7']. The CI is added as projected_eu7_ci to each OK candidate's JSON record and surfaced as a proj_eu7_ci column (+ a transparency annotation) in report.md. The gate path (build_probe_dicts -> candidate_passes -> decision_rule_083.probe_15a_pass) still receives the POINT only; decision_rule_083.py was NOT touched.
commits: a8738dd8 test(0.8.3/Slice 15a fix-6): RED — projected_eu7 transparency CI in output
965b5bc0 fix(0.8.3/Slice 15a fix-6): emit projected_eu7 bootstrap CI (design §5.4 transparency; gate still POINT) (codex §9 P2)

implementation.per_query_series: projected_eu7_recalls(doc_vecs, query_vecs, *, hamming_k, top_k=10, mean_center=True) -> list[float] now holds the previous projected_eu7 body and returns the per-query recall list ([] for empty inputs). projected_eu7(...) calls it and returns float(np.mean(recalls)) (0.0 if empty) — the gated point, numerically identical to before.
implementation.ci_compute: In run_probe's metric loop, proj_recalls = projected_eu7_recalls(docs, qvecs, hamming_k=PROJECTED_EU7_K, mean_center=True); proj = float(np.mean(proj_recalls)) if proj_recalls else 0.0. proj_recalls is stored in per_model_raw[name]['projected_eu7_recalls']. In the per-candidate assembly loop, proj_eu7_ci = paired_bootstrap_ci(proj_recalls, [0.0]*len(proj_recalls), seed=bootstrap_seed, n_resamples=bootstrap_resamples) — single-series reuse of the SAME helper used for eu8/hard CIs (paired delta vs a zero baseline => point == mean(recalls)). Added 'projected_eu7_ci': proj_eu7_ci to the OK candidate record alongside the unchanged 'projected_eu7' point.
implementation.report_md: Per-candidate table gains a proj_eu7_ci column rendering [lo, hi] (4dp) for OK candidates and '—' for failed ones; a one-line annotation states the CI is transparency-only and the gate consumes the POINT (proj_eu7 >= 0.90).

new: test_run_probe_emits_projected_eu7_transparency_ci — runs the torch-free tiny run_probe harness; asserts every OK candidate record carries projected_eu7_ci with keys {lo,hi,point}, all finite, lo<=point<=hi, point == pytest.approx(rec['projected_eu7']) (the gated point), and that a second run with the same fixed bootstrap seed yields a byte-identical CI (determinism).
tests.import_added: test file gained `import math` (used by the new test's math.isfinite checks).
tests.red_confirmed: At RED commit a8738dd8 on baseline fix-5 code: 1 failed, 22 deselected for -k transparency_ci — AssertionError 'bge-base missing projected_eu7_ci' (no such field on current code).
tests.green: FATHOMDB_TESTS_NO_REBUILD=1 PYTHONPATH=src/python .venv/bin/python -m pytest src/python/tests/test_s15a_embedder_probe.py -q -m 'not integration' -p no:cacheprovider => 19 passed, 4 deselected (18 prior + 1 new). All prior tests stay green; the 4 torch integration tests remain deselected.

gate_unchanged: decision_rule_083.probe_15a_pass reads cand['projected_eu7'] (the POINT) >= EU7_FLOOR. build_probe_dicts still constructs the cand dict from the POINT only; projected_eu7_ci is never passed to the gate. probe_15a_pass / EU7_FLOOR / decision_rule_083.py / the gold were NOT modified. The gate verdict is byte-for-byte what it was pre-fix-6.
pyright: 0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 informations on both changed files (pyright 1.1.409, PYTHONPATH=src/python canonical .venv python).
determinism: Numerics of vectors/eu8/hard/the gate are UNCHANGED. projected_eu7 returns the identical mean it always did (only refactored to reuse the per-query series). The new CI uses the existing deterministic paired_bootstrap_ci with the fixed bootstrap_seed and the same n_resamples as the eu8/hard CIs (fixed seed => identical CI across runs, asserted by the new test). The committed measurement result (dev/plans/runs/0.8.3-s15a-embedder.json/-report.md) stays numerically valid; only an additive transparency CI field/column would appear on a regenerate, so a --full re-run is at the orchestrator's discretion (no numeric change).
